diff_summary: Wallet aging report (Story 27.4): pure reducer packages/catalog/src/wallet-aging.ts (aggregateWalletAging + 5 buckets), DB read wallet-aging-db.ts (loadWalletAging), API apps/api/src/routes/admin/wallet-aging.ts (GET report + GET /export with audit), contracts in packages/contracts/src/index.ts (query schema, CSV, view-model, hrefs), admin lib + page. Outstanding invoices aged by createdAt into 0-7/8-30/31-60/61-90/90+ buckets with per-parent rows, CSV export, accountant-gated.
ac_coverage: AC1 (buckets 0-7/8-30/31-60/61-90/90+) implemented & tested (boundary ages 0/7/8/30/31/60/61/90/91/365 all asserted in wallet-aging.test.ts). AC2 (per-parent rows under each bucket, clickable to parent profile via userId->/parents/:userId/statement) implemented & tested. AC3 (CSV export) implemented & tested + audited (report.wallet_aging.export). Authz tested (accountant/admin/super_admin/treasury 200, reception 403, unauth 401). All ACs covered.

findings.title: Aging report includes settled_on_credit invoices — the known double-count: same debt counted as both a negative wallet balance and an aged outstanding amount
findings.detail: loadWalletAging filters `invoices.status NOT IN ('settled','void') AND amount_due > 0` (packages/catalog/src/wallet-aging-db.ts:48). This does NOT exclude `settled_on_credit`. When a check-in resolves settled_on_credit (auto-credit overdraw), packages/wallet/src/debit.ts:205-230 posts a debit ledger row (amount: -amount) that drives the wallet balance NEGATIVE and sets only status/updatedAt — it never zeroes amount_due. FIFO top-up settlement (packages/wallet/src/settle.ts:124) only consumes invoices WHERE status='pending', so a settled_on_credit invoice is never picked up and keeps its full positive amount_due forever. Net effect on this report: (1) the outstanding amount is double-counted — once as the parent's negative wallet balance (the real debt) and once as an aged invoice here; the grand total therefore overstates true AR. (2) Worse for an aging report specifically: after the parent repays via a top-up (wallet balance returns to 0+), the settled_on_credit invoice STILL appears in aging with its full amount_due and an ever-growing age, a phantom outstanding line that never clears and drifts into the 90+ bucket. This contradicts the report's own stated definition (wallet-aging.ts:9-14 claims the figures reconcile to the canonical outstanding definition). This is a pre-existing, cross-cutting bug in the platform's 'outstanding' definition, NOT introduced by S04 — the identical filter is used by apps/api/src/routes/parents/wallet.ts:27, reception/parent-profile.ts:67, reception/parents-search.ts:162 and packages/catalog/src/operations-dashboard-db.ts:92 (the 27.1 dashboard), and it was already flagged in code-review findings P1-E05-S02 and P2-E07-S02. S04 faithfully reproduces the established (flawed) definition. No test exercises a settled_on_credit invoice in wallet-aging.test.ts or the API test (only 'outstanding' and 'settled' statuses are seeded), so the gap is entirely unaddressed.
findings.fix: Out of scope for S04 — needs a platform-wide product/payments decision applied consistently across all sites that share the outstanding definition (parents/wallet.ts, reception/parent-profile.ts, reception/parents-search.ts, operations-dashboard-db.ts, and here). Options: (a) exclude settled_on_credit from the outstanding filter [NOT IN ('settled','void','settled_on_credit')] so the credit debt is represented once, as the negative wallet balance; or (b) zero amount_due (and/or close the invoice) at the moment debit.ts transitions an invoice to settled_on_credit, so amount_due reflects only genuinely-open invoices. Either way add a wallet-aging test that seeds a settled_on_credit invoice and asserts it is NOT aged. Track as the same cross-cutting item as P1-E05-S02 / P2-E07-S02; do not patch in this story alone (would diverge from the siblings).

findings.title: asOf is interpreted as UTC midnight while the business runs in EAT (UTC+3) — invoices created 00:00-03:00 EAT on a boundary day can land in the adjacent bucket
findings.detail: The API maps the YYYY-MM-DD asOf to UTC start-of-day: `new Date(`${asOf}T00:00:00.000Z`)` (apps/api/src/routes/admin/wallet-aging.ts:38-41), and ageDays floors the millisecond diff to whole days (packages/catalog/src/wallet-aging.ts:116-119). Kenya is UTC+3 with no DST, so the report instant is effectively 03:00 EAT on the chosen day. For an invoice whose createdAt falls in the 00:00-03:00 EAT window of a day that is exactly on a bucket boundary (age = 7, 8, 30, 31, 60, 61, 90, or 91 days when measured from local midnight), the UTC-anchored measurement yields an age up to 3 hours larger, which floor() can tip into the older/adjacent bucket vs. what an accountant computing from local calendar days would expect. The bucket math itself is correct (contiguous inclusive ranges, no off-by-one); this is purely the EAT-vs-UTC anchoring of 'today'. It is the established codebase convention — revenue-by-period-db.ts:50 and the reconciliation export both anchor YYYY-MM-DD to T00:00:00.000Z — so it is consistent, not a regression.
findings.fix: Defer — matches the platform-wide UTC-midnight date convention (revenue-by-period, reconciliation export). If exact local-day aging is wanted, anchor asOf and ages to Africa/Nairobi (e.g. T00:00:00+03:00) consistently across all reporting date handling, not just this report. Product/finance call on whether the 3-hour skew matters for AR aging.
